div.span: if it's broken, i'm already fixing it
i have a bambu lab x1c and a problem with starting new projects
good tools make people faster. i try to build good tools.
just wanted a cleaner install process. built a protocol handler.
i once built a pool controller. my dad still uses it.
the kind of guy who adds a web portal to a keychain
got tired of spreadsheets at gaming fairs
yes it has a web portal. no i don't know why.
german engineering. self-inflicted.
building an open-source robot arm. currently in pieces.
i care a lot about the things i make
maker. engineer. whatever it takes.
from PCB to deploy script in one person
i automate things i could just do manually. it's a gift.
i built a dev dashboard because i didn't like my other dev dashboards
documentation: written, occasionally read
the hot tub still works btw
once saw a problem at an event. now it's a SaaS.
i built a stream deck plugin that deploys my own SaaS
it compiles. mostly.
i read the datasheet. all of it.
built in NRW, shipped to the world
i write docs because future-me is grateful
built crewvolution so gaming events could stop using excel
git blame says i did that. git blame is correct.
added one more feature. and then one more. and then one more.
yes, those are different overlays. yes, i made them myself.
the boring parts of a project are still my problem
my home lab has more uptime than most startups
built a full streaming setup. purely for vibes.
i test things. sometimes before shipping.
powered by curiosity and too much coffee and energy drinks
powered by energy drinks and a concerning amount of open tabs
if something annoyed me once, i'll fix it so it never does again
i told myself i'd keep it simple this time
